summary: PR 2735 reorganized Nana fighter code by shrinking ftNn_Init.c to the init range, splitting former ftNn_Init code into ftNn_SpecialHi.c and ftNn_SpecialS.c, updating splits/symbols/configure, and adding a SpecialHi header. The bot report shows linked code/data improvements and 39 new matches, with the apparent broken matches mainly corresponding to functions moved out of ftNn_Init into new units.

key_files.role: Reduced to ftNn_Init range, removed SpecialHi/SpecialS-related bodies and stale includes/prototypes/data declarations.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: ftNn_Init.c text range removed from 0x801230D0 onward; only short prototypes remain
diff_excerpt: removed includes for ef/eflib.h, ftCo_Fall, ftCo_FallSpecial, ft_081B, ft_0892, ftanim, ftcommon, lb_00B0, pl/player

key_files.role: New Nana SpecialHi translation unit receiving functions from the old ftNn_Init range.
evidence_refs: splits.txt diff: ftNn_SpecialHi.c text start 0x801230D0 end 0x801238E4, sdata2 0x804D98A0-0x804D98C0
bot report: multiple new matches in main/melee/ft/chara/ftNana/ftNn_SpecialHi, including ftNn_Init_801230D0, ftPp_SpecialHi_4_Coll, ftNn_Init_801237F8

key_files.role: New Nana SpecialS translation unit receiving later functions/data from the old ftNn_Init/ftNn_Unk0 range.
evidence_refs: splits.txt diff: ftNn_SpecialS.c text start 0x801238E4 end 0x801243AC, data 0x803CDD60-0x803CDD78, sdata2 0x804D98C0-0x804D98F0
configure.py diff: ftNn_SpecialS.c added as NonMatching
bot report: ftNn_SpecialS has new matches for ftNn_Init_80123954, ftPp_SpecialS_1_Anim, ftPp_SpecialS_0_Anim, .data, .sdata2 and near-matches for SpecialS collision functions

key_files.role: Old placeholder/unknown unit emptied or removed after its data moved into the SpecialS split.
evidence_refs: splits.txt diff: ftNn_Unk0.c split entry replaced by ftNn_SpecialHi.c and ftNn_SpecialS.c entries

key_files.role: Old unknown header reduced/removed with the split rename.
Updated text/data/sdata2 ownership boundaries for ftNn_Init, new ftNn_SpecialHi, and new ftNn_SpecialS units.
evidence_refs: splits.txt diff: ftNn_Init text end changed 0x801243AC to 0x801230D0
splits.txt diff: ftNn_SpecialHi.c and ftNn_SpecialS.c entries added

key_files.role: Adjusted local/global symbol metadata around Nana data and sdata2 after the unit split.
evidence_refs: symbols.txt diff: ftNn_Init_804D9898 through ftNn_Init_804D98BC replaced with local @225, @241, @242, @275, @281, @326, @340, @341 symbols

key_files.role: Updated matching status and build object list for Nana split units.
evidence_refs: configure.py diff: ftNn_Init.c changed from NonMatching to Matching
configure.py diff: ftNn_SpecialHi.c added as Matching
configure.py diff: ftNn_SpecialS.c added as NonMatching

what_changed.text: The original ftNn_Init.c split range was narrowed from text 0x80122EDC-0x801243AC to 0x80122EDC-0x801230D0, leaving init content in ftNn_Init.c.
evidence_refs: ftNn_Init.c diff: 578-line function block removed after ftNn_Init_8012309C

what_changed.text: A new ftNn_SpecialHi.c unit was introduced for text 0x801230D0-0x801238E4 and marked Matching in configure.py.
A new ftNn_SpecialS.c unit was introduced for text 0x801238E4-0x801243AC, data 0x803CDD60-0x803CDD78, and sdata2 0x804D98C0-0x804D98F0, but remained NonMatching in configure.py.
ftNn_Unk0 split ownership was replaced by the named SpecialHi/SpecialS units.
Symbol metadata for several ftNn_Init sdata2 objects was converted from global address-style names to local @ labels, and a local data label was added at ftNn_Init_MotionStateTable.
The bot report recorded +0.07% linked code, +0.12% matched data, +0.11% linked data, 39 new matches, and two unmatched-item SpecialS collision improvements around 99.9%.

smart_moves.text: The PR aligned translation-unit boundaries with behavior names: init code stayed in ftNn_Init, SpecialHi behavior moved to ftNn_SpecialHi, and SpecialS behavior moved to ftNn_SpecialS.
The build configuration was updated at the same time as the split, preventing the new source files from being orphaned from matching status.
Function movement reduced unnecessary includes in ftNn_Init.c after SpecialHi/SpecialS bodies left the file.

The bot report's broken matches for ftNn_Init correspond closely to new matches in ftNn_SpecialHi and ftNn_SpecialS, suggesting the report should be read as a relocation/split effect rather than a simple regression.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl bot report: new matches and broken matches lists

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a large fighter init TU contains contiguous special-move behavior ranges, splitting by move-specific unit can turn an apparently nonmatching or overloaded file into smaller units with clearer matching ownership.
evidence_refs: bot report: 39 new matches and ftNn_Init.c changed to Matching

decomp_lessons.lesson: After splitting a TU, update splits.txt, symbols.txt, configure.py, and headers together; otherwise symbol ownership and matching status may not reflect the new source layout.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl lists config, symbols, configure, source, and header changes

decomp_lessons.lesson: Progress reports after a split can show many broken matches in the old unit and corresponding new matches in the new unit; intake should compare item names and units before treating these as real regressions.
evidence_refs: bot report: ftNn_Init broken matches mirror new matches in ftNn_SpecialHi and ftNn_SpecialS

naming_conventions.text: Move-specific Nana files use ftNn_SpecialHi and ftNn_SpecialS names, replacing the less descriptive ftNn_Unk0 split entry.
Unresolved function symbols remained address-style, such as ftNn_Init_801230D0 and fn_80123218, rather than being given speculative semantic names.
Some sdata2 objects were demoted from global ftNn_Init_804D98xx names to local @ labels after split ownership changed.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Primary tactic was translation-unit splitting and split-boundary adjustment, not local source-shape steering: ftNn_Init text ended at 0x801230D0, ftNn_SpecialHi covered 0x801230D0-0x801238E4, and ftNn_SpecialS covered 0x801238E4-0x801243AC.

configure.py was used to declare ftNn_Init.c and ftNn_SpecialHi.c as Matching while leaving ftNn_SpecialS.c NonMatching, matching the bot report where SpecialS collision functions were still only near-matches.
Symbol table scope/label changes accompanied the split, including a local label for ftNn_Init_MotionStateTable and local @ labels for early Nana sdata2 constants.

review_feedback.text: decomp-dev[bot] reported GALE01 improvements: linked code 48.61% (+0.07%, +2568 bytes), matched data 47.92% (+0.12%, +1404 bytes), linked data 41.10% (+0.11%, +1332 bytes), 39 new matches, 35 broken matches, two unmatched-item improvements, and two unmatched-item regressions.
No human review comments or review records were loaded for this PR slice.

follow_up_queries.reason: Find the follow-up PR that completed the remaining near-matches in the new NonMatching SpecialS unit.
Locate related history around retiring the unknown Nana split file.
Check whether later symbol/data curation kept or revised the local sdata2 label decisions.
Review header/prototype ownership after the new SpecialHi unit was introduced.

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR identity, merge state, changed files, counts
raw_diff/diff_excerpt: splits.txt, symbols.txt, configure.py, ftNn_Init.c changes
changed_files.jsonl: exact touched files and line counts
notes: PR body was empty, so author rationale is inferred only from title and diff structure.
The raw diff is truncated in the loaded slice; details of the full new ftNn_SpecialHi.c, ftNn_SpecialS.c, and header contents are not fully visible.
No human review feedback was present in the loaded evidence.
Bot report proves reported matching percentages for this PR slice but does not prove manual validation beyond the automated report.
The term 'Link ftNn_Init' is preserved from the PR title, but the actual diff also introduces a NonMatching ftNn_SpecialS unit.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Interpret match reports carefully after TU splits
accepted_candidate_records.text: After moving functions from one translation unit into new split units, bot reports may show old-unit broken matches alongside new-unit matches for the same symbols. Compare unit and item names before treating the old-unit losses as true regressions.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl bot report: ftNn_Init broken matches mirror ftNn_SpecialHi/ftNn_SpecialS new matches
splits.txt diff: ftNn_Init range split into ftNn_SpecialHi and ftNn_SpecialS

accepted_candidate_records.title: Split overloaded fighter init files by move-specific ownership
accepted_candidate_records.text: For fighter code, contiguous behavior ranges embedded in an init file can be split into move-specific TUs when split boundaries and build configuration support it. In this PR, ftNn_Init was narrowed while ftNn_SpecialHi and ftNn_SpecialS received the moved ranges.

source_update_candidates.title: ftNana source layout after PR 2735
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2735 split the former ftNn_Init range so ftNn_Init.c owns text 0x80122EDC-0x801230D0, ftNn_SpecialHi.c owns text 0x801230D0-0x801238E4 with sdata2 0x804D98A0-0x804D98C0, and ftNn_SpecialS.c owns text 0x801238E4-0x801243AC with data 0x803CDD60-0x803CDD78 and sdata2 0x804D98C0-0x804D98F0.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful path-scoped history for future Nana split/config work; should be curated against current source before accepting.

source_update_candidates.title: ftNn_SpecialS remained NonMatching in PR 2735
source_update_candidates.text: Although many SpecialS functions/data became matched in the new ftNn_SpecialS unit, configure.py added ftNn_SpecialS.c as NonMatching, and the bot report still showed ftPp_SpecialS_0_Coll and ftPp_SpecialS_1_Coll as near-matches rather than full matches.
source_update_candidates.reason: May help future searches for the follow-up that completed SpecialS collision matching; current status must be checked before promotion.

source_update_candidates.title: Treat split-induced broken matches differently from regressions
source_update_candidates.text: When a PR changes split ownership, matching reports can list broken matches in the old unit and new matches in the new unit for the same symbols. Reviewers should compare moved item names and split ranges before classifying those report entries as real regressions.
evidence_refs: bot report: 35 broken matches in ftNn_Init and corresponding new matches in ftNn_SpecialHi/ftNn_SpecialS
source_update_candidates.reason: This is a possible global review heuristic, but one PR is only limited evidence; curator should decide whether it belongs in standards or just path facts.

rejection_notes.text: Do not infer semantic names for address-style functions such as ftNn_Init_801230D0 from this PR; the diff preserves address-style names and provides no naming rationale.
Do not claim all Nana SpecialS code was matched by this PR; configure.py marks ftNn_SpecialS.c NonMatching and the bot report lists two SpecialS collision functions as only 99.88-99.89%.
Do not treat the added symbols.txt local @ labels as a global convention for all sdata2 constants; this PR only shows local changes for this Nana split.
